channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Executions nearly doubled in the U.S. last year, and soared abroad
item.description: The number of people killed in state-sanctioned executions worldwide rose to a 44-year high in 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International.
item.content:encoded:
The number of people killed in state-sanctioned executions worldwide rose to a 44-year high in 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International.

(Image credit: Curt Anderson)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Military aircrew in 'stable condition' following midair collision at Idaho air show
item.description: Two jets appeared to collide and their crews ejected during a performance at an air show at a military base in Idaho. "The aircrew involved in the incident are in stable condition," the base reported.
item.content:encoded:
Two jets appeared to collide and their crews ejected during a performance at an air show at a military base in Idaho. "The aircrew involved in the incident are in stable condition," the base reported.

(Image credit: Senior Airman Xavier Wilson)
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach

item.title: Trump and administration officials address Christian gathering on National Mall
item.description: The conservative prayer event was organized by Freedom 250, a public-private group working with the White House to organize the celebration of America's 250 birthday this summer.
item.content:encoded:
The conservative prayer event was organized by Freedom 250, a public-private group working with the White House to organize the celebration of America's 250 birthday this summer.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: San Francisco celebrates iconic peace activist Wavy Gravy's 90th birthday
item.description: The peace activist, counterculture icon and philanthropist known as Wavy Gravy turned 90, and San Francisco threw a big party.
item.content:encoded: The peace activist, counterculture icon and philanthropist known as Wavy Gravy turned 90, and San Francisco threw a big party.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Monahan

item.title: Bus riders to Montgomery retrace old steps while fighting a new fight
item.description: Thousands of people rallied Saturday in Montgomery, Alabama, to push back against conservative states' efforts to dismantle congressional districts that helped secure Black political representation.
item.content:encoded:
Thousands of people rallied Saturday in Montgomery, Alabama, to push back against conservative states' efforts to dismantle congressional districts that helped secure Black political representation.

(Image credit: Mike Stewart)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: After deadly floods last year, over 300 Texas summer camps await licenses to open
item.description: Texas has tightened standards for camps after deadly flooding last July. With summer camp season about to start, only nine camps are approved to open and more than 300 are awaiting their licenses.
item.content:encoded: Texas has tightened standards for camps after deadly flooding last July. With summer camp season about to start, only nine camps are approved to open and more than 300 are awaiting their licenses.
item.dc:creator: Leigh Walden

item.title: New burn bans and Trump's battle with immigration and DEI are impacting forest fires
item.description: Firefighters say setting fires on purpose is one of the best ways to protect against massive wildfires later. But the Trump administration is banning or stalling preventative burning across the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
Firefighters say setting fires on purpose is one of the best  ways to protect against massive wildfires later. But the Trump administration is banning or stalling preventative burning across the U.S.

(Image credit: Chiara Eisner)
item.dc:creator: Chiara Eisner

item.title: Louisiana senator who voted to convict Trump loses Republican primary
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy is one of few remaining Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump after the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol. Trump endorsed his opponent, Rep. Julia Letlow.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Bill Cassidy is one of few remaining Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump after the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol. Trump endorsed his opponent, Rep. Julia Letlow.

(Image credit: Annie Flanagan for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Napoleon Solo holds off Iron Honor to win the Preakness
item.description: It's the colt's first victory of the year. Kentucky Derby winner Golden Tempo's exit from the race left the field of 14 horses wide open to contest the middle jewel of the Triple Crown.
item.content:encoded:
It's the colt's first victory of the year. Kentucky Derby winner Golden Tempo's exit from the race left the field of 14 horses wide open to contest the middle jewel of the Triple Crown.

(Image credit: Nick Wass)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: producing a great interview
item.description: The team behind NPR's Wild Card explains how careful preparation helps them produce interviews that reveal deep and surprising human moments.
item.content:encoded: The team behind NPR's Wild Card explains how careful preparation helps them produce interviews that reveal deep and surprising human moments.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Geiran
